evals.prompt: PROJ-123 can you check if this story is testable?
evals.expected_output: Asks the user for the ticket key or fetches PROJ-123, then produces a structured analysis report with testability issues, ISTQB quadrant coverage, missing test scenarios, and improved AC. Should not assume field names or AC format.

evals.prompt: analyze story AUTH-456, we're going into refinement tomorrow and I want to know if the AC is solid before the session
evals.expected_output: Fetches AUTH-456, performs full analysis, identifies any ambiguous or untestable AC items, maps to testing quadrants, and provides a refinement-ready report with rewritten/new AC and clear recommended actions.

evals.prompt: is CHECKOUT-89 ready for QA? the dev says it's done but i'm not sure the AC covers everything
evals.expected_output: Fetches CHECKOUT-89, reviews AC coverage, identifies missing edge cases (especially for checkout flows: empty cart, payment failure, concurrency), maps to quadrants, and produces a clear 'Ready / Needs refinement / Not ready' verdict with reasoning.

Fetches the story or uses AC already in context, derives test cases grouped by criterion covering positive, negative, edge cases, and non-functional. No Gherkin, no test IDs. Ends with a coverage summary and suggests /assess-risk.

evals.prompt: what should I test for the password reset flow? AC: user enters email, receives a reset link, link expires after 24h
evals.expected_output: Produces test cases for all three AC points — happy path (valid email, link received), negative (invalid email, expired link, link already used), and edge cases (email with special chars, multiple requests). Pre-conditions and expected results are clear.

evals.prompt: Prioritise these test areas before our release: payment checkout, user profile update, email notifications, admin dashboard
evals.expected_output: Scores each area by likelihood and impact, produces a risk matrix sorted descending, and outputs a phased execution order. No test IDs. Asks if prioritisation matches the user's understanding.

evals.prompt: what should I test first for PROJ-123?
evals.expected_output: If test cases are in context uses them; if not, scores at feature-area level using the story's AC. Produces a ranked matrix and recommended execution order.

evals.prompt: Explore https://app.example.com and create a manual test plan. Username is qa@example.com, password is Test1234!
evals.expected_output: Navigates to the URL, logs in with the provided credentials, explores all reachable sections, and produces a structured manual test plan grouped by feature area with happy path, negative, and edge case scenarios. No Gherkin, no test IDs.

evals.prompt: I need a test plan for our staging environment at https://staging.myapp.io — it's public, no login needed. Focus on the checkout flow.
evals.expected_output: Navigates to the URL without authentication, focuses exploration on the checkout flow while noting other visible areas, and produces a test plan scoped to checkout with happy path, payment failure, validation errors, and edge cases like empty cart or session expiry.

evals.prompt: Can you map out what we should manually test on https://demo.app.com? Login: admin / admin123. After that, create a Jira ticket in the QA project.
evals.expected_output: Explores the app authenticated as admin, produces a full manual test plan, then asks about Jira and creates a Story in the QA project with the test plan as description and posts it as a comment.

evals.prompt: I found a bug — when I submit the login form with an empty password field the page just refreshes with no error message shown
evals.expected_output: Structures a bug report with a precise summary, severity (Medium), steps to reproduce, expected result (validation error shown), actual result (silent page refresh), and offers to create a Jira bug ticket.

evals.prompt: the checkout flow broke in staging — after clicking Pay nothing happens and the console shows a 500 error
evals.expected_output: Classifies as Critical/Urgent, asks for any missing context (browser, reproducibility), writes a concise report quoting the 500 error, and creates a Jira bug linked to the relevant story if one was mentioned.

evals.prompt: log this defect: user profile photo upload silently fails on Safari but works on Chrome. Steps: go to profile, click upload, select a jpg, click save. Expected: photo updates. Actual: spinner appears then disappears, photo unchanged.
evals.expected_output: Uses the provided steps directly without re-asking, classifies severity as Medium (workaround: use Chrome), writes a complete report with browser-specific context noted, and offers to file in Jira.
